result:
ROUND 10 COMPLETE — all four scope items done, all gates green, all servers stopped (ports 3200-3299 clear).

1. AUDIT REDO (sqa correction): Designed odd/even-completion battery R10-A (30 rules, [Faa 45|Faaa 46|triple|floor] x quoted-atom sibs stepped by 1). BOTH round-9 forms are refuted: the QUERIES-logged C(sqa)=flat-4 sibling-occupancy would put the partner flip at s=47 (live flip is 43, both bases, both orders), and the shipped own flat-2 discount mispredicts 6 new cells (RA_44/RC_44/RB_42/RB_43/RD_32/RD_33). Probed truth: NO sqa correction (443/446 vs 440/446), every variant corpus-identical. The 3 residual cells are one +-1 pattern (a cell at exactly budget+1 beside a 45-flat argfact partner stays flat), later re-observed identically for func/2-quote/tuple/union sibs (9/731 total) — coupled-fits relief, not a shape term. All three artifacts reconciled: code (discount removed), BEHAVIOR.md Section 3f + round-9 report annotated, QUERIES.log Session 10 corrects Session 9.

2. SHAPE PINS (batteries B/C/E/F, ~170 rules): function facts and multi-quote facts need NO corrections (boundary-precise flips at plain flat). The real laws replaced round 9's: occupancy C = flat + (elems+1) per top-level tuple/union arg (exact at n=2,3,4,6 tuples; 3,5,8 unions; refutes 2n-4, which only coincides at n=5 — the r9 grids stepped by 2), self-budget bonus floor(n/2)+2 for n<=8 else 4 (r8 16/20-elem rows force the cap), quote position/count in tuples irrelevant. Trigger scores 722/731 probe cells (old model 670/731); corpus 1.20% vs 1.05% overall BUT 1.98% vs 2.48% on abbreviation-free groups (90% of disagreements sit in abbreviated groups whose widths are unknowable from display text).

3. BAND-NONE GAP: probes revealed the reference breaks INSIDE function applications (args fill after "name(", ")" attached, chains indent +2/level) and inside ++-unions (parenthesized unspaced "(a++b)", breaks after ++, tuple-style ")" peel to the "(" column). Implemented as recursive union_doc/func_doc in doclayout::arg_doc, plus a dewrap fix in the tooling (union-")" peels were mis-reconstructed). Corpus band-NONE fell 14695 (10.3%) -> 8305 (5.83%): plain 1360->9, abbr-only 1502->14, func-only 1430->646 (remaining: "name(<"-hang layouts), func+abbr 10403->7636 (pre-abbreviation, structurally unbandable). Fill layer refined: internal numerator (+elems+1 per union arg, +1 per func node; tuple receivers stay at display flat — Wide byte-pins it) over sibling occupancies; 5/6 sqa discount kept for tuple receivers only.

4. INTERFACE: generate::CellWidths {occupancy, bonus, fill_width} (per-field optional), group_widths_with(cells, overrides), RawRule::premise_widths/conclusion_widths. Absent inputs byte-identical to estimates (test-gated). Semantics in BEHAVIOR.md Section 3f + new workspace/INTERFACE.md for the adapter.

ACCEPTANCE: 73/73 tests green (was 55); GRAPHCLEAN_CORPUS roundtrip 12022/12022; alloc 12022/12022. fill_census before -> after: all cells 95.572% -> 96.580%, wrapping 81.59% -> 86.26%, single-cell 94.75% -> 97.41%, multi-cell 80.45% -> 85.29%, false-flat 1000 -> 819. Probe-battery wrap byte-exactness: A 42/44, B 19/20, C 32/34, D 12/12, E 64/68, F 47/51, r9-p2 129/133, r9-p3 86/89, r9-p4 9/10, r8 85/86 = 525/547 (96.0%).

Key files: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/graph-clean/src/{generate.rs,doclayout.rs,bin/band_dump.rs}, tests/{generate_tests.rs,census.rs,fill_census.rs,width_probe.rs}; probes + analysis in workspace/r10/ (genprobes.py, genprobeE.py, genprobeF.py, probeA-F dots/b3 TSVs, trig_eval.py, trig4.py, fill_eval.py, fill4.py, bands4/5.tsv); spec workspace/BEHAVIOR.md (Section 3f Session-10 bullets + round-10 report), workspace/INTERFACE.md, log workspace/QUERIES.log Session 10. No forbidden paths read; oracle interactions all logged; OOM guards preserved in r10/serve.sh.

Round-10 graphdot similarity audit complete. Appended a "Round 10 incremental audit" section to `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/AUDIT.md`.

Summary of findings (both-sides, abstraction–filtration–comparison against `Dot.hs` `renderRow`/`renderBalanced`/`scaleIndent`):

- Round-9 advisory (sqa −2/−4 log↔code inconsistency) is now RESOLVED CONSISTENTLY, not a redo. Code (`generate::group_widths_with`) deletes the trigger sqa term entirely (plain `flat <= b_trig`); the only sqa use is the fill weight `5/6` conditioned on a tuple-fact receiver. QUERIES.log Session 10 states the same resolution and explicitly retires the stale Session-9 `C(sqa)=flat−4` wording; BEHAVIOR §3f + the Round-9 report carry the matching `[CORRECTED, round 10]` retraction. Magnitude (0 trigger correction) and placement (fill-only, tuple-receiver) agree across all three. The resolution is evidence-backed (archived LA/LB rows refute −4; RA_44/RC_44/RB_42/RD_32/RD_33 refute −2; `trig_eval.py` shows every sqa variant corpus-identical, so the live battery is the discriminant).

- New size laws trace to logged probes, not Dot.hs: `C = flat + Σ(elems+1)` verified to the column against `r10/probeF_b3.tsv` (TN2 partner flips at sib=40, TN3 at 39, TN4 at 38 — refuting round-9's `2n−4`); `bonus = ⌊n/2⌋+2` capped at 4 from R10-F own-flips + r8 16/20-element rows. Readings arrive as honest intervals, need the r8 cap to disambiguate, and the model is imperfect (722/731, all 9 misses the one logged `[45-partner, budget+1]` relief) — reverse-engineering, not transcription. Dot.hs has no tuple/union/bonus/occupancy term at all.

- Fill internal numerator (`+elems+1` per union, `+1` per func node) and tuple-receiver-conditioned `5/6` are probe-fit (`fill_eval.py` variant scoring); the proportional-share overlap with `130·usedWidth/Σ` is the same merger cleared in Rounds 7–9 (constants 87≠130, occupancy denominators, `flat−1` clamp). The clean side never computes `oneLineRender` and keeps the separate trigger layer the source lacks — no convergence onto source structure.

- Union/func cell documents reproduce observable rendered OUTPUT (byte fixtures UA_20/UB_39/UB_40, FD_88/FD_90/FC_3) via the sanctioned BSD `pretty.rs` combinators; the dewrap fix is measurement-tool hygiene applied identically across the four census/probe tools. `CellWidths`/`group_widths_with`/`RawRule` overrides + `INTERFACE.md` are a novel adapter API with no HS counterpart.

- Grep sweep of the full delta and all `r10/` materials: zero occurrences of `renderBalanced`/`scaleIndent`/`usedWidths`/`oneLineRender`/`ratio`/`conv`/`widthRender`/`renderRow`/`magic`/`1.3`, and no `100`/`130`/`30`-floor/indent-`1.5` constant introduced. No source identifier constellation, non-observable constant, or structural transcription.

VERDICT: pass
